evals.prompt: Help me start writing my paper on dynamic community detection.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 starts; the agent refuses to draft prose until the user has a one-sentence contribution, asks the target venue type once, and works on the contribution sentence.

evals.prompt: Here is my intro skeleton: Contributions: (1) We propose a dynamic graph neural method for evolving communities, supported in Section 3. (2) We prove a stability guarantee, supported in Section 4. (3) We provide the first benchmark for temporal community drift. Sections: 1 Introduction, 2 Related Work, 3 Method, 4 Theory, 5 Experiments. Run the Phase 1 gate.
evals.expected_output: The Phase 1 gate fails because the benchmark contribution has no supporting section; the orphan contribution is quoted and a repair is requested.

evals.prompt: Run the math prose pass on this theorem: Assume G is connected, has maximum degree Delta, and p > 1/2. Theorem. The recovery algorithm succeeds with probability at least 1-epsilon.
evals.expected_output: Phase 3 flags that hypotheses live outside the theorem statement, quotes the theorem, and rewrites it as a self-contained theorem statement.

evals.prompt: Should I keep the abstract really short to get cited more?
evals.expected_output: A direct evidence-flagged answer: [REFUTED] ultra-short abstracts are not supported, citing Weinberger, Evans, and Allesina 2015, plus [SUPPORTED] math/physics shorter-sentence nuance.

evals.prompt: I have a complete mini-draft for a graph ML conference paper: contribution sentence, method, experiments, theorem appendix, related work, limitations, and code repo. Help me prepare it for submission.
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 produces a draft handoff report with venue fit, draft integrity, coauthor sign-off, reproducibility artifacts, and handoff recommendations to argument-audit, ai-writing-detector, and submission-readiness-audit.
